commands.repair_reason: Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is live Windows/WSL2 proof or explicit maintainer acceptance, and the PR carries the protected maintainer label.; Cleared: The diff narrows Windows process termination for managed node services and does not add dependencies, workflow permissions, secrets handling, package resolution, or other supply-chain surfaces. (sha=9204441152acbc9ace41eff1ed369eed053f3935)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Windows Scheduled Task daemon handling so node service lifecycle commands skip Gateway listener cleanup and instead identify the installed node host by command-line matching, with added stop/startup fallback regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main lets the shared Windows Scheduled Task stop/restart path parse the node task's Gateway port and run Gateway listener cleanup even though the node service wrapper sets OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND=node. I did not run a live Windows/WSL2 reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is live Windows/WSL2 proof or explicit maintainer acceptance, and the PR carries the protected maintainer label.

target.title: fix(daemon): keep node tasks off gateway listener cleanup
target.body:
Summary
- Keep Windows node Scheduled Task stop/restart/status from treating the gateway listener port as node-owned runtime evidence.
- Add installed-command process matching for node Scheduled Task and Startup-folder fallback paths, so node lifecycle commands can still find the managed node host without touching unrelated gateway listeners or manual node runs.
- Cover registered task, Startup fallback, mixed fallback/task, and delayed schtasks launch cases.

Fixes #85289

Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `codex review --uncommitted`

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Windows node Scheduled Task lifecycle no longer reclaims or reports runtime from the Gateway listener port, while still detecting and stopping the installed node host process by matching the installed command line.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at /root/src/openclaw-85289 with mocked Windows Scheduled Task/process behavior; no destructive live Windows node service restart was run against the active VM gateway.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: The verification commands listed above were run after the final patch.
Evidence after fix: `schtasks.stop.test.ts` passed 7/7 and `schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts` passed 27/27; final Codex review reported no discrete correctness issues.
Observed result after fix: Node service paths skip gateway listener/port cleanup, ignore unrelated manual node hosts on the same gateway port, and terminate only the matching installed node host command when stop/restart needs process cleanup.
What was not tested: destructive live Windows service stop/restart against the active Sebby/OpenClaw VM setup.

Additional maintainer verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.test.ts src/daemon/service.test.ts src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts` - passed 5 files / 78 tests
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox: `tbx_01ks9f2fmhfar6ahwqvx071pak`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26322648137, command `corepack pnpm check:changed`, exit 0
